authMethod.description: Select the authentication method to use when connecting to Jira

cloudId.description: Jira App Cloud Id is required to be used with API Token with Scopes

hosting.description: The provider is where your JIRA instance is hosted. Select Jira Cloud if your Jira is being hosted on Atlassian Cloud. Select Jira Data Center / Jira Server if your Jira is being hosted elsewhere.

oauthClientID.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID for the Jira service account

scopedToken.description: Toggle this switch on if API Token with scopes is used

serverName.description: Server name used for TLS SNI / certificate verification. Optional; overrides the hostname used to verify the returned certificate.

tlsAuth.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

tlsAuthWithCACert.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

tlsSkipVerify.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

url.description: The URL is the root URL for your Atlassian instance (example: company.atlassian.net)

user.description: The email address for the user/service account

secureValues.description: The API token is the token associated with the user. You can create one here.
The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret for the Jira service account
The client certificate is encrypted and stored in the Grafana database.
The client key is encrypted and stored in the Grafana database.
